perturbation.original_text: 7. Termination. This AGREEMENT shall expire on Dece mber 31, 2017 . Notwithstanding the foregoing, each PARTY that receives or obtains CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION pursuant to the terms of this AGREEMENT shall remain bound for five (5) years from the date of disclosure by all confidentiality obligations, and su bject to all remedies, under this AGREEMENT after termination, including, without limitation, the obligations set forth in Sections 3 , 6 and 7 and the remedies specified in Section 4. Upon written request, all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION shall be returned to the DISCLOSING PARTY or destroyed at the request of the DISCLOSING PARTY. Similarly, if at any time prior to December 31, 2017 , a PARTY withdraws from the NRSC , any DISCLOSING PARTY may request that the withdrawing PARTY, and the withdrawing PARTY may re quest that the RECEIVING PARTY either return all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to the DISCLOSING PARTY or destroy such information. Within seven (7) days of receipt of a written notice requesting return or destruction of any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, the RECEI VING PARTY shall, as requested, either return or destroy all designated CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION and all copies of such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, whether in written or electronic form.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Termination. This AGREEMENT shall expire on Dece mber 31, 2017 . Notwithstanding the foregoing, each PARTY that receives or obtains CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION pursuant to the terms of this AGREEMENT shall remain bound for one (1) year from the date of disclosure by all confidentiality obligations, and su bject to all remedies, under this AGREEMENT after termination, including, without limitation, the obligations set forth in Sections 3 , 6 and 7 and the remedies specified in Section 4. Upon written request, all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION shall be returned to the DISCLOSING PARTY or destroyed at the request of the DISCLOSING PARTY. Similarly, if at any time prior to December 31, 2017 , a PARTY withdraws from the NRSC , any DISCLOSING PARTY may request that the withdrawing PARTY, and the withdrawing PARTY may re quest that the RECEIVING PARTY either return all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to the DISCLOSING PARTY or destroy such information. Within seven (7) days of receipt of a written notice requesting return or destruction of any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, the RECEI VING PARTY shall, as requested, either return or destroy all designated CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION and all copies of such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, whether in written or electronic form.
perturbation.explanation: This creates an in-text contradiction by changing the duration of confidentiality obligations post-termination from five (5) years to one (1) year. This contradicts the initial clause which states five (5) years of confidentiality, leading to uncertainty on what exact year does the terms of the agreement, as the the confidentiality obligations, last. Which of the numbers is being applied, and when does the agreement effectively end.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Termination. This AGREEMENT shall expire on Dece mber 31, 2017 . Notwithstanding the foregoing, each PARTY that receives or obtains CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION pursuant to the terms of this AGREEMENT shall remain bound for one (1) year from the date of disclosure by all confidentiality obligations, and su bject to all remedies, under this AGREEMENT after termination, including, without limitation, the obligations set forth in Sections 3 , 6 and 7 and the remedies specified in Section 4. Upon written request, all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION shall be returned to the DISCLOSING PARTY or destroyed at the request of the DISCLOSING PARTY. Similarly, if at any time prior to December 31, 2017 , a PARTY withdraws from the NRSC , any DISCLOSING PARTY may request that the withdrawing PARTY, and the withdrawing PARTY may re quest that the RECEIVING PARTY either return all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to the DISCLOSING PARTY or destroy such information. Within seven (7) days of receipt of a written notice requesting return or destruction of any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, the RECEI VING PARTY shall, as requested, either return or destroy all designated CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION and all copies of such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, whether in written or electronic form.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the duration of the confidentiality obligation from five (5) years to one (1) year post-termination. This directly contradicts the initial clause in both the changed text and contradicted text, which states that the confidentiality obligations last for five (5) years, according to the original text. This inconsistency creates a direct conflict as to which duration is applicable, leading to uncertainty and a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Nondisclosure; Ownership of Proprietary Property . (a) Each PARTY her eby acknowledges that it is in the best interests of the other PARTIES to insist on the confidentiality of any and all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION disclosed pursuant to this AGREEMENT. (b) Each PARTY reserves the right to decide not to disclose any CONFIDEN TIAL INFORMATION, and nothing herein shall require the disclosure of any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION by any PARTY. It is understood that any disclosures undertaken pursuant to the AGREEMENT are voluntary and do not constitute a waiver of any rights or privileges with respect to future or other disclosures. (c) The PARTIES hereby agree that the DISCLOSING PARTY shall retain ownership and control over any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION disclosed pursuant to this AGREEMENT. The RECEIVING PARTIES shall not by reason of any disclosure made pursuant to this AGREEMENT thereby obtain any rights with respect to such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION other than the right of the PARTY, or an ENTITY that the PARTY assigns its rights to under this Agreement in compliance with Section 5 herein, to use the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION for the purposes of NRSC analysis only, and agree that they will not: (i) use such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION for any 2 reason other than NRSC analysis only as expressly authorized by this Agreement; or (ii) sell, lend, lease, distribute, license, give, transfer, assign, show, disclose, disseminate, appropriate, or otherwise communicate any such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any person or ENTITY outside the RECEIVING PARTY or its assignee under this Agreement, except as expressly authorized under this AGREEMENT.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Nondisclosure; Ownership of Proprietary Property . (a) Each PARTY her eby acknowledges that it is in the best interests of the other PARTIES to insist on the confidentiality of any and all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION disclosed pursuant to this AGREEMENT. (b) Each PARTY is REQUIRED to disclose any CONFIDEN TIAL INFORMATION, and nothing herein shall allow the PARTY to refrain from disclosure of any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION by any PARTY. It is understood that any disclosures undertaken pursuant to the AGREEMENT are MANDATORY and constitute a waiver of any rights or privileges with respect to future or other disclosures. (c) The PARTIES hereby agree that the DISCLOSING PARTY shall retain ownership and control over any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION disclosed pursuant to this AGREEMENT. The RECEIVING PARTIES shall not by reason of any disclosure made pursuant to this AGREEMENT thereby obtain any rights with respect to such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION other than the right of the PARTY, or an ENTITY that the PARTY assigns its rights to under this Agreement in compliance with Section 5 herein, to use the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION for the purposes of NRSC analysis only, and agree that they will not: (i) use such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION for any 2 reason other than NRSC analysis only as expressly authorized by this Agreement; or (ii) sell, lend, lease, distribute, license, give, transfer, assign, show, disclose, disseminate, appropriate, or otherwise communicate any such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any person or ENTITY outside the RECEIVING PARTY or its assignee under this Agreement, except as expressly authorized under this AGREEMENT.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. Originally, the parties had the right to not disclose it, as the disclosures were voluntary. By modifying it, it is now mandatory, and the waiver of any rights now applies, in contradictory to the other parts of the text. The perturbation creates uncertainty and could lead to disputes over whether the agreement is voluntary or obligatory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Nondisclosure; Ownership of Proprietary Property . (a) Each PARTY her eby acknowledges that it is in the best interests of the other PARTIES to insist on the confidentiality of any and all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION disclosed pursuant to this AGREEMENT. (b) Each PARTY is REQUIRED to disclose any CONFIDEN TIAL INFORMATION, and nothing herein shall allow the PARTY to refrain from disclosure of any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION by any PARTY. It is understood that any disclosures undertaken pursuant to the AGREEMENT are MANDATORY and constitute a waiver of any rights or privileges with respect to future or other disclosures. (c) The PARTIES hereby agree that the DISCLOSING PARTY shall retain ownership and control over any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION disclosed pursuant to this AGREEMENT. The RECEIVING PARTIES shall not by reason of any disclosure made pursuant to this AGREEMENT thereby obtain any rights with respect to such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION other than the right of the PARTY, or an ENTITY that the PARTY assigns its rights to under this Agreement in compliance with Section 5 herein, to use the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION for the purposes of NRSC analysis only, and agree that they will not: (i) use such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION for any 2 reason other than NRSC analysis only as expressly authorized by this Agreement; or (ii) sell, lend, lease, distribute, license, give, transfer, assign, show, disclose, disseminate, appropriate, or otherwise communicate any such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any person or ENTITY outside the RECEIVING PARTY or its assignee under this Agreement, except as expressly authorized under this AGREEMENT.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that parties had the right to not disclose confidential information, making disclosures voluntary. The changed text makes disclosure of confidential information mandatory. This directly contradicts the original agreement by removing the voluntary nature of disclosure, creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined by 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'. The contradiction is present because the changed text is in the same location as the Contradicted text, which reinforces the strong in-text contradiction and its potential to cause confusion, unenforceability, or legal issues within the document.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Binding Effect and Assignability . The rights and obligations of each PARTY under this AGREEMENT shall inure to the benefit of and shall be binding upon any subsidiary, affiliate, successor or permitted assign of or to the business of such PARTY, to the extent provided below. Neither this AGREEMENT nor any rights or obligations of a PARTY under this AGREEMENT shall be transferable or assignable by that PARTY without the prior written consent of all other PARTIES, and any attempted transfer or assignment of this 3 AGREEMENT by a PARTY not in accordance herewith shall be null and void. Notwith standing the foregoing provisions of this Section, a PARTY may assign this AGREEMENT immediately, without the prior written consent of all other PARTIES, (a) to any ENTITY that c ontrols, is controlled by, or is in common control with the assigning PARTY, ( b) to any successor in interest to the assigning PARTY, or (c) if necessary to satisfy the rules, regulations and/or orders of any federal, state or local governmental agency or body. The co nfidentiality obligations and remedies set forth in this AGREEMEN T, including without limitation, those set for in Sections 3, 4, 5, 6 and 7 shall bind any successor to any PARTY. Notwithstanding the foregoing, each PARTY that receives or obtains CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION pursuant to the terms of this AGREEMENT shall re main bound by all confidentiality obligations, and subject to all remedies, under this AGREEMENT after making an assignment or transfer, including, without limitation, the obligations set forth in Sections 3, 6, 7 and 8 and the remedies specified in Sectio n 4.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Binding Effect and Assignability . The rights and obligations of each PARTY under this AGREEMENT shall inure to the benefit of and shall be binding upon any subsidiary, affiliate, successor or permitted assign of or to the business of such PARTY, to the extent provided below. Neither this AGREEMENT nor any rights or obligations of a PARTY under this AGREEMENT shall be transferable or assignable by that PARTY WITH the prior written consent of all other PARTIES, and any attempted transfer or assignment of this 3 AGREEMENT by a PARTY not in accordance herewith shall be valid. Notwith standing the foregoing provisions of this Section, a PARTY may NOT assign this AGREEMENT immediately, without the prior written consent of all other PARTIES, (a) to any ENTITY that c ontrols, is controlled by, or is in common control with the assigning PARTY, ( b) to any successor in interest to the assigning PARTY, or (c) if necessary to satisfy the rules, regulations and/or orders of any federal, state or local governmental agency or body. The co nfidentiality obligations and remedies set forth in this AGREEMEN T, including without limitation, those set for in Sections 3, 4, 5, 6 and 7 shall bind any successor to any PARTY. Notwithstanding the foregoing, each PARTY that receives or obtains CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION pursuant to the terms of this AGREEMENT shall re main bound by all confidentiality obligations, and subject to all remedies, under this AGREEMENT after making an assignment or transfer, including, without limitation, the obligations set forth in Sections 3, 6, 7 and 8 and the remedies specified in Sectio n 4.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the assignability of the agreement. It initially states that the agreement shall be assignable with prior consent. Next, it is stated that an attempted transfer will be valid. After that, it states that the parties are NOT allowed to assign the agreement under ANY circumstances. These contradictions creates a high risk of uncertainty of what is actually applicable. In this case, this introduces doubt and uncerainty around the agreement's conditions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Binding Effect and Assignability . The rights and obligations of each PARTY under this AGREEMENT shall inure to the benefit of and shall be binding upon any subsidiary, affiliate, successor or permitted assign of or to the business of such PARTY, to the extent provided below. Neither this AGREEMENT nor any rights or obligations of a PARTY under this AGREEMENT shall be transferable or assignable by that PARTY WITH the prior written consent of all other PARTIES, and any attempted transfer or assignment of this 3 AGREEMENT by a PARTY not in accordance herewith shall be valid. Notwith standing the foregoing provisions of this Section, a PARTY may NOT assign this AGREEMENT immediately, without the prior written consent of all other PARTIES, (a) to any ENTITY that c ontrols, is controlled by, or is in common control with the assigning PARTY, ( b) to any successor in interest to the assigning PARTY, or (c) if necessary to satisfy the rules, regulations and/or orders of any federal, state or local governmental agency or body. The co nfidentiality obligations and remedies set forth in this AGREEMEN T, including without limitation, those set for in Sections 3, 4, 5, 6 and 7 shall bind any successor to any PARTY. Notwithstanding the foregoing, each PARTY that receives or obtains CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION pursuant to the terms of this AGREEMENT shall re main bound by all confidentiality obligations, and subject to all remedies, under this AGREEMENT after making an assignment or transfer, including, without limitation, the obligations set forth in Sections 3, 6, 7 and 8 and the remedies specified in Sectio n 4.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an in-text contradiction within Section 5 of the agreement regarding its assignability. Originally, the agreement was not transferable or assignable without prior written consent, with exceptions for specific situations like assignment to entities under common control. The changed text first states that assignments are valid with prior written consent, then contradicts itself by stating that a party may NOT assign the agreement immediately without prior written consent, even in the previously allowed circumstances. This is a direct conflict because the initial section allows for assignment under certain conditions, while the modified section prohibits it entirely, even under those same conditions. As the Changed Text and Contradicted Text are the same section in the same location, the contradiction is very clear.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Remedies: Damages, Injunctions and Specific P erformance . The PARTIES expressly understand and agree that the covenants and agreements to be rendered and performed by the PARTIES pursuant to this AGREMEENT are special, unique, and of a n extraordinary character, and in the event of any default, or bre ach by any PARTY of this AGREEMENT, the affected PARTY shall have no adequate remedy at law and shall be entitled to seek such relief as may be available to it pursuant hereto, at law or in e quity, including, without limiting the generality of the foregoin g, any proceedings to: (i) obtain direct damages for any breach of this AGREEMENT; (ii) order the specific performance thereof; or (iii) enjoin the breach of such provisions. This AGREEMENT shall be governed by the laws of the State of New York without r egard to its choice of law principles. With respect to the participation of any governmental ENTITY, employee or officer in this AGREEMENT, federal law shall apply.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Remedies: Damages, Injunctions and Specific P erformance . The PARTIES expressly understand and agree that the covenants and agreements to be rendered and performed by the PARTIES pursuant to this AGREMEENT are NOT special, unique, and of a n extraordinary character, and in the event of any default, or bre ach by any PARTY of this AGREEMENT, the affected PARTY shall have an adequate remedy at law and shall NOT be entitled to seek such relief as may be available to it pursuant hereto, at law or in e quity, including, without limiting the generality of the foregoin g, any proceedings to: (i) obtain direct damages for any breach of this AGREEMENT; (ii) order the specific performance thereof; or (iii) enjoin the breach of such provisions. This AGREEMENT shall be governed by the laws of the State of New York without r egard to its choice of law principles. With respect to the participation of any governmental ENTITY, employee or officer in this AGREEMENT, federal law shall apply.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the remedies available in case of a breach. By saying that the agreement IS NOT special and unique, the legal rights afforded from the text change. The main contradiction comes when, in the original text, the offended party had NO adequate remedy at law, but now DOES. This is highly risky.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Remedies: Damages, Injunctions and Specific P erformance . The PARTIES expressly understand and agree that the covenants and agreements to be rendered and performed by the PARTIES pursuant to this AGREMEENT are NOT special, unique, and of a n extraordinary character, and in the event of any default, or bre ach by any PARTY of this AGREEMENT, the affected PARTY shall have an adequate remedy at law and shall NOT be entitled to seek such relief as may be available to it pursuant hereto, at law or in e quity, including, without limiting the generality of the foregoin g, any proceedings to: (i) obtain direct damages for any breach of this AGREEMENT; (ii) order the specific performance thereof; or (iii) enjoin the breach of such provisions. This AGREEMENT shall be governed by the laws of the State of New York without r egard to its choice of law principles. With respect to the participation of any governmental ENTITY, employee or officer in this AGREEMENT, federal law shall apply.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text regarding the availability of remedies. The original text states that the parties would have no adequate remedy at law, implying equitable remedies like specific performance are available. The changed text asserts the opposite, stating there IS an adequate remedy at law and equitable remedies are NOT available. Since the contradicted text is the same as the changed text, this creates a clear and direct contradiction within the document itself, specifically about the remedies available upon breach of contract, as the affected party had NO adequate remedy at law, but now DOES.

perturbation.original_text: 8 Severability . All sections and subsections of this AGREEMENT are severable, and the unenforceability or invalidity of any of the sections or subsections of this AGREEMENT shall not affect the validity or enforceability of the remaining sections or su bsections of this AGREEMENT, but such remaining sections or subsection s shall be interpreted and construed in such a manner as to carry out fully the intention of the PARTIES.
perturbation.changed_text: 8 Severability . All sections and subsections of this AGREEMENT are NOT severable, and the unenforceability or invalidity of any of the sections or subsections of this AGREEMENT shall affect the validity or enforceability of the remaining sections or su bsections of this AGREEMENT, but such remaining sections or subsection s shall be interpreted and construed in such a manner as to carry out fully the intention of the PARTIES.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the agreement's parts are severable, and if one section is invalid, the remaining one is still valid. The change makes it the opposite, and now if one part is wrong, the whole thing is considered wrong and invalid. The main concern of the contradictions is what is actually going to be considered in case one party does not fulfill the conditions of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8 Severability . All sections and subsections of this AGREEMENT are NOT severable, and the unenforceability or invalidity of any of the sections or subsections of this AGREEMENT shall affect the validity or enforceability of the remaining sections or su bsections of this AGREEMENT, but such remaining sections or subsection s shall be interpreted and construed in such a manner as to carry out fully the intention of the PARTIES.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the agreement sections are severable, while the changed text explicitly states they are NOT severable. The contradicted text repeats the changed text, but the initial change from 'are' to 'are NOT' in the severability clause creates a direct contradiction. If one part is invalid, the original text ensures the rest remains valid; the changed and contradicted text state that the entire agreement becomes invalid, causing a strong inconsistency concerning the agreement's enforceability and therefore, a contradiction.
